sections.paragraph:
διχοστασία, ας, ἡ (s. two next entries; Solon 3, 37 Diehl2; Bacchylides 11, 67 BSnell [’34]; Hdt. 5, 75; Dionys. Hal. 8, 72, 1; Plut., Mor. 479a; Michel 448, 19; 1 Macc 3:29; TestSol 18:16; Ps.-Phocyl. 151; SibOr 4, 68) the state of being in factious opposition, dissension (w. ἐριθεῖαι, αἱρέσεις) Gal 5:20; cp. 1 Cl 46:5; 51:1. διχοστασίας ποιεῖν cause dissensions Ro 16:17; cp. Hs 8, 7, 5; 8, 10, 2; 1 Cor 3:3 v.l.; Hv 3, 9, 9; m 2:3.—DELG s.v. δί and ἵστημι. M-M.

Solon Solon , VII–VI b.c.—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Bacchylides Bacchylides , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 252
